channel.title: Raleigh NC Parks and Recreation News
channel.description: Latest news from Raleigh Parks and Recreation

item.title: LeadHERship Basketball Clinic (Mar 30, 2026)
item.description:
Worthdale Community Center



News Published 3/30/2026 Updated 3/30/2026



Worthdale Community Center, in partnership with Paradigm Sports Academy Inc., is proud to host the first session of LeadHERship Basketball Clinic!



This all-girls clinic is all about leveling up, building skills, boosting confidence, and deepening your understanding of the game in a fun, supportive environment. Whether you're sharpening your fundamentals or learning new moves, this is your time to shine!



Location: Worthdale Community Center 
Date: Saturday, April 4
Time: 11 a.m. - 2 p.m.
Ages: 13 - 18 
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink



Worthdale Park



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Breakfast with the Bunny and Egg Hunt (Mar 30, 2026)
item.description:
Saturday, April 4 at Biltmore Hills



News Published 3/30/2026 Updated 3/30/2026



You will even have the opportunity to take a picture with the Biltmore Bunny. Breakfast is at 10 a.m., and the Egg Hunt will start at 11 a.m. This event is both indoor and outdoor.



Time: 10 a.m.
Ages: All
Cost: $5 per person
Register on RecLink



Time: 11 a.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Pre-registration is not required



Biltmore Hills Park
2615 Fitzgerald Drive
Raleigh, NC 27610



919-996-6895



Biltmore Hills Park



Feb 17, 2026



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Share Your Voice in the Biltmore Hills Master Plan Update (Mar 30, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 3/30/2026 Updated 3/30/2026



Community members are encouraged to participate either in person or online. Public input will help inform the design and shape the long-term vision for the expansion and enhancement of Biltmore Hills Park.



A Design Alternates Public Meeting will be held on Tuesday, April 14, from 4 to 6 p.m. at the Biltmore Hills Community Center. This family-friendly public workshop will give participants the opportunity to learn about the project, compare the design alternates developed for the Biltmore Hills Park Expansion, and share feedback on specific park amenities and layouts that matter most to them.



For those unable to attend in person, an online survey will be available from April 6 through April 20, providing another way to share input and participate in the planning process.



Accommodation and translation services are available upon request. Please email BiltmoreHillsUpdate@publicinput.com for assistance.



More information about the project, engagement opportunities, and online participation is available on the project engagement portal.



Location: Biltmore Hills Community Center
Date: Tuesday, April 14
Time: 4 - 6 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free



April 14, 2026 4:00PM



BiltmoreHillsUpdate@PublicInput.com



Biltmore Hills Park



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Lunchbox Lecture with Cheetie Kumar (Mar 13, 2026)
item.description:
She has been profiled in The New York Times, Southern Living, and The Wall Street Journal, and many other national publications.



Join us for an in-depth Q&A on Cheetie’s career, food advocacy work, and upcoming projects in the Raleigh restaurant scene.

item.title: Wake County Senior Games (Mar 13, 2026)
item.description:
Senior Games are Olympic-style sporting events held at the local, state, and national levels across the United States, engaging more than 100,000 participants ages 50 and older each year. The Games offer the camaraderie and excitement of friendly competition while promoting health, wellness, and active lifestyles for older adults. Participants are inspired to stay active, challenge themselves, and connect with others in their community.



The Wake County Senior Games would not be possible without the time, energy, and dedication of our incredible volunteers. Their support helps create a welcoming and encouraging environment for participants. Most importantly, volunteers help bring joy, motivation, and plenty of smiles to the athletes who compete.



Volunteer opportunities for the upcoming season are now available, and we would love your help. Visit www.wcseniorgames.com/volunteer to view the full list of opportunities available from March through May. Each event listing includes a brief description of the volunteer role along with dates, times, and locations.



All volunteers will receive a T-shirt, and snacks will be provided at each event. Volunteers under 18 years of age must be accompanied by an adult. Please remember that a volunteer waiver must be completed and brought with you for each event.



For more information or questions, please contact us at 919-996-4735 or wakecountyseniorgames@gmail.com



Join us in supporting the Wake County Senior Games and help make a difference in the lives of active older adults in our community.



Dates: March 27 - May 19
Ages: 15 and up
Volunteer with Wake County Senior Games

item.title: Springtime Wreath Workshop (Mar 20, 2026)
item.description:
You will receive step-by-step guidance on how to arrange and attach decorations to the base, and learn techniques to achieve a visually appealing and balanced design. You will leave the class with a one-of-a-kind wreath!



You are encouraged to bring your own decorations (3-4), ribbon, and greenery if you have a certain theme in mind. Otherwise, we will have some decorations available. All other supplies and tools will be provided.



Location: Five Points Center for Active Adults
Date: Friday, April 17
Time: 1 - 4 p.m.
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: $31
Register on RecLink

item.title: Participate in the City Nature Challenge! (Mar 20, 2026)
item.description:
Visit our preserves, parks, community centers, and greenways to find plants, animals (including insects), fungi, and other living things. Download the free iNaturalist app in the Apple or Android App Stores to submit photos of the things you see. Instructions on How to Make an Observation.



You can participate anywhere in Raleigh parks, preserves, and greenways, our community centers, downtown, or anywhere in the world! By documenting your observations with iNaturalist, you will learn about the living things around you and connect with a community of other people who value learning about nature. You will also help guide natural resources management decisions and contribute to scientific studies locally and internationally, while competing to find more species than other cities.



Location: Anywhere (Raleigh parks, preserves, and greenways, our community centers, downtown, or anywhere in the world)
Dates: Friday, April 24 - Monday, April 27
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Instructions on How to Make an Observation

item.title: Wow Mom: Gifts for Mother's Day (Mar 20, 2026)
item.description:
In our two hours we will create a set of candles, a paper flower bouquet, and a little card to tell mom just how amazing she is. We encourage assistance, but adults aren’t required to register unless they will be participating themselves. Registration is required , and this is not a drop-in event. The crafts will take 2 hours.

Location: Lake Lynn Community Center
Date: Saturday, May 9
Time: 10 a.m. - Noon
Age: 5 and older
Cost: $15
Register on RecLink

item.title: City of Oaks Open Pétanque Tournament (Mar 20, 2026)
item.description:
Register your team of two or three players for three one-hour rounds in the morning, a break for lunch, and then single elimination in the afternoon. Bring your enthusiasm and get ready to point and shoot!



Location: Pullen Park - Upper Shelter Area
Date: Saturday, April 25
Check-In: 8:30 a.m.
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: Free
Registration Form

item.title: Teens Wanted: Build a Comic with Us! (Mar 12, 2026)
item.description:
Work with artist Wolly McNair (who's worked with Nike, Archie Comics, IDW Comics) and help shape Raleigh’s new emergency preparedness comic book.



Share your ideas and tell us what characters, colors, and symbols make you feel safe and represented. Then create your own comic character and bring your hero to life. Come hungry, get creative, and be part of a project designed by teens, for teens!



If you can't attend the event, please take our survey.



Location: Peach Road Park
Date: Saturday, March 28
Time: noon - 2 p.m.
Ages: Teens
Cost: Free
Reserve Your Spot

item.title: Spring Black Neighborhood Walking Tour (Mar 12, 2026)
item.description:
Put some spring in your step as you explore Raleigh's historic and influential Black neighborhood, the Third Ward, just around Shaw University, a wonderful place full of culture, business, and learning.



Be sure to register to join in the fun! Saturday tour times are 10 a.m. and 2 p.m., and Sunday tour time 2 p.m.



Saturdays and Sundays, March 21 - June 21
Saturdays, 10 a.m. & 2 p.m.
Sundays, 2 p.m. 
Ages: All
Cost: $10 per adult / $5 per child (5- 17)
Register on RecLink

item.title: Make-A-Zine Workshop! (Mar 12, 2026)
item.description:
Youth will explore papercrafts, collage, drawing, and writing to create personal pieces about their own interests and collaborative pieces with the class as a whole.

At the end of each program, the class will have a showcase of their work for parents/guardians to see their finished zines.

item.title: Adult Basketball Registration (Mar 27, 2026)
item.description:
You may register online or in person at the Athletics Division office located at 2401 Wade Avenue, Raleigh, NC 27607. In-person registration hours are Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 5:15 p.m.



If you are not currently part of a team, you are encouraged to join the league by being added to the Free Agent list. To be placed on the list, please contact City of Raleigh Athletics. For additional information or assistance with registration, call 919-996-6836 or email athletics@raleighnc.gov. 



The Adult Basketball League offers a great opportunity for you to stay active, build camaraderie, and enjoy friendly competition.



Locations: Abbotts Creek, Carolina Pines, Halifax, Jaycee, Laurel Hills, Lions, and Marsh Creek
Game Days: Monday - Thursday
Ages: 18 and up
Cost: $450 Team
Register on RecLink

item.title: Youth Summer Basketball Registration (Mar 27, 2026)
item.description:
The City of Raleigh Athletics Division will be offering outdoor youth basketball for ages 9-12 (as of August 31, 2025). League age groups, 9-10 and 11-12, will be offered as an outdoor basketball league at the Baileywick Park Outdoor Pavilion.  



Open registration will be held from April 1-14. Interested athletes may register online or at any City Parks community center during the registration period.  The Athletics Program will provide the general equipment for practices and games, as well as team uniforms.  



For more information about youth basketball, please contact Athletics by visiting the Athletics website, calling 919-996-6836, or emailing athletics@raleighnc.gov. 



Registration: April 1 - 14
Ages: 9 to 14 years old (League age as of Aug. 31, 2025)
Fee: $65 for Residents / $80 for Non-Residents
Register on RecLink beginning April 1

item.title: Planting Power Exhibition Closing Reception (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
Planting Power: 100 Years of the Raleigh Garden Club explores a century of history of this major civic organization. Since its founding in March 1925, the Raleigh Garden Club has played a vital role in shaping the city’s green spaces. Its founding mission to create “a garden with a city in the heart of it” has resulted in a century of notable plantings and projects. Visit the exhibit and learn the story of how individual volunteers, in what began as an all-woman garden club, had a major impact on Raleigh's appearance for one hundred years and counting. 



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Wednesday, April 15
Time: 10 - 11 a.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Did You Know Raleigh Parks Uses Prescribed Burns? (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
This video provides an inside look at how and why trained staff use controlled, carefully planned burns as a land management tool. Prescribed burns help reduce the buildup of dry vegetation, lower the risk of uncontrolled wildfires, and promote the growth of native plants.



“Prescribed burns are a critical part of how we care for our natural areas,” said Brian Smith, Natural Resources Manager. “They allow us to safely manage vegetation, support wildlife habitats, and keep our parks resilient for the future.”



In addition to improving safety, prescribed burns play a key role in restoring natural ecosystems. Many native plant species depend on fire to thrive, and these burns help maintain the balance needed for diverse and healthy habitats.



The video also emphasizes the planning and expertise involved in each burn. Controlled burns are led by trained and experienced Raleigh Parks staff and supported by trained professionals from agencies such as the NC Forest Service. Strict safety guidelines are used, including the monitoring of weather conditions, to minimize impacts from smoke. As Raleigh continues to grow, proactive land management practices like prescribed burns help ensure that parks remain safe, sustainable, and enjoyable for generations to come.

item.title: Little Sprouts Event at Lake Lynn (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
Designed especially for our curious Pre-K learners, this event is a celebration of the wonders of Spring. Families will enjoy hands-on activities, seasonal crafts, and a nature scavenger hunt while supplies last.



Partnering with Leesville Library, there will also be a library craft and two story times at 10:30 a.m. and 11:30 a.m. Registration is encouraged , but not required.



Location: Lake Lynn Park
Date: Monday, May 11
Time:  10 a.m. - noon
Ages: 3-5 years old
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Soccer Teacher Workday Camp (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
In the morning, they’ll spend two hours learning and practicing skills with coaches, then enjoy friendly soccer games, team challenges, and traditional camp activities. It’s a full day of fun for beginners or soccer fans alike!



Time: 9 a.m. to 5 p.m.
Ages: 6 - 11
Cost: Resident: $80 (Non-Resident: $95)
Register on RecLink

item.title: Lunchbox Lecture with Julia Einstein (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description: A local painter known for delving into the study of floral history to fill her garden and studio with inspiration. Julia will walk us through the growing season as inspiration for her large flower icon paintings!

item.title: Family Fun Day Squirrels! (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
Come to the City of Raleigh museum and celebrate all things related to our favorite woodland creature. Make squirrel headbands, search for acorns, and even take a picture with our very own mascot, Sammy the Squirrel!



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Saturday, April 11
Time: 11 a.m. - 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
No Registration Required

item.title: Drawing in the Galleries (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
Well, this may be your best chance! Join us for a still-life drawing session using our own objects as reference materials.



We will provide the lights and the action. You will need to bring any sketching supplies. Please no paints or liquid materials. This is a ticketed event for 21 and over. Learn more and get tickets !



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Friday, Apr 17
Time: 7 - 9 p.m.
Ages: 21 and older
Cost: $20
Learn More and Get Tickets

item.title: Beyond The Book: Children’s Book & Art Festival (Mar 11, 2026)
item.description:
Discover interactive performances, collaborative art installations, creative crafts, talented vendors, artisan food, and inspiring entertainment. Join us for a day immersed in stories that will spark your imagination, and don’t forget to dress as your favorite book character! 

Beyond The Book Children’s Book & Art Festival is open to all with books & activities designed for all ages. 

Chapter 5 theme to be announced soon!



Location: Fred Fletcher Park
Date: Saturday, April 11
Time: 10 a.m. - 2 p.m.
Age: All 
Cost: Free

item.title: Partial Reopening of Pullen Amusements Begins April 1 (Mar 26, 2026)
item.description:
Beginning April 1, Pullen Park Amusement rides, attractions, playground, and amenities will reopen while work continues on the Lake Howell shoreline improvement project. We’re excited to welcome visitors back to many of the park’s favorite attractions this spring and summer.



Most amusements and the main park areas will be open and operating. Visitors will see construction activity near the lake, and some brief, early morning and weekday closures may be necessary for materials delivery.



Know Before You Go



What Remains Closed



We appreciate your understanding as we make these necessary improvements to help keep Pullen Park safe, accessible, and enjoyable for years to come. We encourage you to explore other Raleigh Parks while improvements are underway. 



For updates, visit our project webpage or follow Raleigh Parks on social media.

item.title: Memoir and Personal Essay Writing Workshop (Mar 26, 2026)
item.description:
It can be funny, touching, or life-changing. Whether you know what you'd like to write about or you need help finding it, this class will have various tools and exercises to help you along the way. 

At the end of 6 weeks, all students will have a 2-3 page story that is personal to them, and a wonderful treasure to share with loved ones.

Location: Five Points Center for Active Adults
Date: Wednesdays, July 8 - Aug. 12 
Time: 11 a.m. - Noon
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: $18 Residents / $33 Non-residents
Register on RecLink

item.title: Historic Oakwood Neighborhood Trolley Tour (Mar 10, 2026)
item.description:
Explore landmark residences and learn about the influential families and visionary builders who shaped this enduring community. This hour-long tour invites people of all ages to explore, learn, and connect with the city’s rich past.



Location: Tour starts and ends at the Tucker House, 418 N. Person St.
Date: Sunday, April 12
Times: 1 p.m., 2 p.m., and 3 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: $12
Purchase Trolley Tickets

item.title: Powell Drive Community Day (Mar 10, 2026)
item.description:
Friday night, we will host a meet and greet with refreshments. Swing by and meet Director Denise Hill and Assistant Director Jason Rodri and pick their brains about programs YOU want to see at Powell Drive.

On Saturday, we will be going all out with inflatables, music, food trucks, a free-throw contest, and more! We will also have many City of Raleigh program area representatives with exciting opportunities to share.

Stop on by for great family fun and enjoy all that the City has to offer!



Friday, April 17, 5 - 8 p.m.
Saturday, April 18, Noon - 4 p.m.
Cost: Free
Ages: All
Share your feedback about Powell Drive Park!

item.title: Green Road Family Sneaker Ball (Mar 25, 2026)
item.description:
This family-friendly event is sure to knock your socks off! Come dressed in semi-formal attire and your coolest kicks.



This event will have an awesome DJ, games, arts and crafts, and food trucks. Let us see your sneaker game while you dance the night away.



Location: Green Road Community Center
Date: Friday, May 1
Time: 6:30 - 9 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Level Up! Mountain Bike Basics (Mar 17, 2026)
item.description:
Our certified instructors will guide you through the fundamentals of mountain biking. Learn and practice riding techniques, body position, braking, and shifting. 

The course will also cover trail etiquette and local mountain biking resources. We'll wrap up with a group trail ride, putting your new skills to use. Bikes and helmets are provided. 

Successful completion of the course qualifies you to participate in the Bike Loaner Program at Forest Ridge Park. Preregistration is required.



Location: Forest Ridge Park
Dates: Saturdays - April 18, May 16, Aug. 22
Time: 9-11 a.m. 
Ages: 14 and up
Cost: $26 Resident / $41 Non-residents
Register on RecLink

item.title: Introduction to Honeybees and Beekeeping (Mar 9, 2026)
item.description:
The honeybee serves an important party in pollinating flowers, especially the flowers that produce our fruits and vegetables. They also provide us honey to eat. 

Come and learn about the honeybee, beekeeping as a hobby, and increase your awareness of this important pollinator in our midst. This is a family friendly program. 

There will be a presentation, hands on stations, and a Q&A at the end. If you an interest in the honeybee or beekeeping, then this is the program for you! Please register each person who plans to attend.



Location: Laurel Hills Park
Date: Saturday, March 28
Time: 10:30 a.m. - 12:30 p.m.
Ages: 9 and up
Cost: $5
Register on RecLink

item.title: Sunset Hike (Mar 9, 2026)
item.description:
We will hike along the northern shoreline of the park, traversing roots and rocks, to pause at the peninsula point for the sunset. With less light on our return trip, you will need a headlamp or flashlight to illuminate your path.



Friday, March 20, 6:30 - 8:30 p.m.
Friday, April 24, 7 - 9 p.m.
Ages: 14 and older
Cost: $6
Register on RecLink

item.title: Food Vendor Program Expansion (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 3/24/2026 Updated 3/24/2026



Adam Forman
adam.forman@raleighnc.gov 



Abbotts Creek Park
Anderson Point Park
Baileywick Park
Buffaloe Road Athletic Park
John Chavis Memorial Park
Lake Johnson Park
Millbrook Exchange Park
Neuse River Greenway Trail
Shelley Lake Park
Walnut Creek Wetland Park
Laurel Hills Park



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Big Sweep at Lake Wheeler Park (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Thank You to our Volunteers



News Published 3/24/2026 Updated 3/24/2026



This week at Lake Wheeler, we were fortunate to have over a dozen volunteers through the Big Sweep program come and pick up trash!



This program is a Wake County initiative in cooperation with the City of Raleigh to help keep our Parks and Green Spaces clean. Our volunteers cleared 30 pounds of small trash that really requires a slow walk through to find.



Thank you so much to all our amazing Volunteers!



Lake Wheeler Park



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Hold the Door Mentorship Program (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Chapman will be hosting a special workshop for male youth ages 12 - 18 on April 19 from 2 – 4 p.m. This workshop will introduce the Hold the Door Mentorship Program and its mission to support and empower young men in the community. 

The event will also feature a keynote address from LeVelle Moton, bringing inspiration and insight to attendees.

This event marks the official kickoff of this impactful new program, aimed at creating opportunities, guidance, and mentorship for youth in the community.



Location: John P. “Top” Greene African American Cultural Center
Date: Sunday, April 19
Time: 2 - 4 p.m.
Ages: 12 - 18 boys
Cost: Free
No Registration is Required

item.title: Roberts Park Spring Celebration! (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Bring the whole family and enjoy an egg-citing day filled with music, snacks, crafts, games, community resources, and an egg hunt. Don't miss your chance to meet Ms. Bunny and snap some sweet photos!



Date: Saturday, April 4
Time: 11 a.m. - 3 p.m.
Ages: 1 - 12
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Celebrate Infertility Awareness Week (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Infertility Awareness Week is observed once a year. This is designed for those who have experienced infertility, those currently navigating the journey, child-free by choice, and anyone who has supported a loved one through it. Take time to slow down, connect, and find a sense of calm in a supportive environment.

Take time to slow down, connect, and find a sense of calm in a supportive environment. 



This inclusive meetup welcomes anyone affected by infertility, navigating it, or child-free by choice. It’s a space to connect, share, and find support in community. Light snacks and small activities for children will be provided. Join us for comfort, conversation, and connection and don’t forget to wear orange in support of the week.

Time: 5:30 - 7 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Let Us Grow: Come Out to Celebrate Earth Day (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Learn all about the parts of a plant, enjoy a story, and get hands-on by planting seeds to take home. A healthy snack with fresh veggies and fruit will be provided. Weather permitting, the program will take place at the outdoor shelter. Please pre-register so we can plan accordingly.



Location: Kiwanis Community Center
Date: Wednesday, April 22
Time: 10 - 11 a.m.
Ages: 0 - 5
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: A Tree’s Journey: In Celebration of Earth Day (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Participants will read My Tree and Me: A Book of Seasons, reflect on a meaningful tree through drawing and sharing, and embody the strength of a tree through playful movement. 

The class concludes with a peaceful forest visualization meditation, helping children feel calm, grounded, and steady like a tree through every season. Guardians are welcome to drop off their child for this class. Please pre-register so we can plan accordingly.



Location: Kiwanis Community Center
Date: Tuesday, April 21
Time: 5:15 - 6:45 p.m.
Ages: 5 - 10
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Barwell Road Community Garden Open House (Mar 16, 2026)
item.description:
Take a stroll, check out the plots, and even pre-select your favorite bed before our official garden rental system launches. Bring your curiosity, maybe your green thumb and let’s get ready to grow together!



Location: Barwell Road Park - The Community Garden is located to the left of the entrance sign
Date: Saturday, April 11
Time: 1 - 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
No registration is required. Parking is available at the garden and near the playground.

item.title: Top Greene’s “Adornment of Grace” Hat Show Contest (Mar 23, 2026)
item.description:
Please join us for The Hat Show Contest, a celebratory event honoring creativity, style, and cultural expression on Sunday, May 17, from 3 – 6 p.m.



We will feature a special guest, Dr. Donna Corbett, and The Mary Corbett Hat Collection will showcase a timeless collection of her mother’s vintage hats, highlighting the enduring elegance and cultural influence of classic millinery. 

From refined silhouettes to bold statements, this presentation honors the legacy of style passed from one generation to the next, and Erica Kendrick, a ceramic artist, sculptor, and educator



Everyone who attends will be entered into the contest and have the opportunity to showcase their hat for a chance to win a FREE 3-hour rental at Top Greene.



Bring your confidence, your culture, and your crown — because the hat makes the statement. Come out, show love, and help us make this a night to remember!

item.title: 8th Annual Mud Day Event! (Mar 23, 2026)
item.description:
Learn about local environmental groups in our area while participating in the fun activities they offer, visit local food trucks, and of course, slide in a giant mud pit! Additional details coming soon!



Saturday, Aug. 15
From 10 a.m. to 1 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Location: 950 Peterson Street, Raleigh, NC 27610
